since_last: 32% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +1.82%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 32% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX -0.01% · BTC -2.84% · Gold +1.53% · Nasdaq100 +0.55%. 32% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +1.82%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +1.82%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -3.06%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +15.55%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +15.55%, AMAT +9.95%, TER +7.72%, KLAC +6.82%, QCOM +4.56%. Laggards: INOD -11.76%, DELL -6.16%, AI -6.06%, OKLO -5.29%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.04% at $152.93
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.04% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
